reasoning: Peer reference sheet shows 12+ Denzi-style helmets/armor rendered with: (1) consistent 1px pure-black outlines on ALL surfaces, (2) cool-to-neutral palette (grays, blue-grays, teal, muted gold), (3) directional shading implying top-left light source, (4) moderate highlight dots on facets, (5) structured pixel facets giving 'chunky cute' charm. Candidate torch_v01: HANDLE — acceptable; dark brown stick with black outlines is close enough to peer rendering language. FLAME — catastrophic mismatch on 6 of 11 dims. Flame is entirely warm-palette (orange/yellow/red) with zero overlap against the cool peer palette; no black outline around flame edges (open pixel clusters); self-emissive appearance with no directional shadow; saturation/brightness of flame pixels dramatically exceeds any highlight found in peers; freeform upward-flowing flame silhouette contrasts with the structured, rigid facet language of every peer sprite; flame lacks the endearing chunkiness of Denzi design — it reads as a generic RPG-maker torch asset. Color temperature divergence alone would be marginal; combined with missing outline, palette isolation, and absent charm, this fails the consistency threshold. Not penalizing for item-type difference (torch vs helmet), but the flame rendering dialect is genuinely incompatible with the peer family style.
